text:
# Page 1^page=1
LOGAN FINNEY MULTIMEDIA JOURNALIST loganﬁnney27@gmail.com 513 S. Curtis Rd Apt 202 208-627-9028 Boise, ID 83705 Logan Finney is an award-winning professional journalist who specializes in covering politics and government. An Idahoan by birth, he grew up hunting, hiking, and skiing in the mountains of Bonner County and remains forever fascinated with the state’s history, culture, and people. Logan studied media history, ethics, and law at the University of Idaho’s School of Journalism and Mass Media, where he gained hands-on experience with production skills for documentary ﬁlm, television news, and digital new media at Idaho Public Television’s studio in Moscow. He now covers Idaho state government and politics for Idaho Reports as a producer and reporter. EMPLOYMENT Idaho Public Television (PBS) 2020-present Producer/Writer 2025-present - Documentary work; journalistic coverage of state government and politics for Idaho Reports. Associate Producer 2021-2025 - Reporting, packages, booking, scriptwriting, graphics, audio, and digital for Idaho Reports. - Filming, interviews, research, and writing for December 2025 episodes of Idaho Experience. Production Assistant 2020-2021 - “COVID-19 Update” scripts, voiceovers, and editing; ongoing pandemic response coverage. EXPERIENCE Idaho Press Club, Member 2020-present - Professional association for working journalists across the state with annual training events. - Recognition for Idaho Reports online presence with “Best Blog” award three years in a row. Society of Professional Journalists, Member 2020-present - Organization dedicated to encouraging the practice of journalism and high ethical standards.

# Page 2^page=2
Investigative Reporters & Editors, Member 2021-present - Professional journalism association dedicated to sharing story ideas, techniques, and sources. - Attendee at NICAR data journalism training conferences (Nashville 2023, Minneapolis 2025). NLGJA: The Association of LGBTQ+ Journalists, Member 2021-present - Media professional network dedicated to journalistic standards in LGBTQ+ issues coverage. Capitol Correspondents Association, Standing Committee Member 2024-present - Organization responsible for credentialing news media and reporters at the Idaho Capitol. - Standing committee manages a shared ofﬁce space and interfaces with legislative leadership. Documentary Producers Alliance, Member 2025-present - Nonproﬁt advocacy organization committed to advancing the documentary ecosystem on behalf of producers for the health of the documentary industry and a fair and just ﬁlm industry. Archival Producers Alliance, Member 2025-present - Industry group to elevate and amplify the role of archival producers within non-ﬁction media as well as promote the value, use, protection, and preservation of authentic archival materials. EDUCATION Digital Transformation Program 2023 Poynter Institute & Corporation for Public Broadcasting - Professional development on digital media initiatives with PBS & NPR afﬁliate station peers. B.S., Broadcasting & Digital Media 2020 School of Journalism and Mass Media, University of Idaho - Regularly included on the dean's list and active member of the University Honors Program. University of Idaho Legislative Reporter 2020 James A. and Louise McClure Center for Public Policy Research - Covered the Idaho Legislature as a student reporter for six local newspapers across the state. Idaho High School Diploma 2016 Sandpoint High School, Lake Pend Oreille School District #84 - Graduated with 4.0+ GPA honors and ranked third academically among the graduating class.

# Page 3^page=3
LOGAN FINNEY MULTIMEDIA JOURNALIST loganﬁnney27@gmail.com 513 S. Curtis Rd Apt 202 208-627-9028 Boise, ID 83705 January 13, 2026 Dear Idaho Press Club scholarship committee, Thank you for considering me for the Idaho Press Club’s 2026 Mid-Career Scholarship. If selected, I would use the $500 award to pay for conference registration at the NLGJA: The Association of LGBTQ+ Journalists 2026 National Convention, Sept. 10-13 in San Francisco. With a loss in funding to Idaho Public Television after the dissolution of the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, I am looking for outside support to help me attend this conference. My employer is also an agency of the State of Idaho, so I want to avoid any possible conﬂict that could arise over government funds going toward me attending an LGBTQ-afﬁnity group event. If awarded the IPC Mid-Career Scholarship, I would use the funds to cover the $495 conference registration fee and then pay for my own travel to San Francisco. A journalist peer who resides in the Bay area has offered to let me stay with him at no cost if I am able to attend. The trainings offered at this annual journalism conference - with 2025 session topics such as “Responsible Reporting on Trans Communities” and “Clashes and Compromises: Inside the Collision of Religious Freedom and LGBTQ+ Rights” and “Out of the Past: Uncovering Local Queer History” - would provide invaluable insight as Idaho continues to legislate queer people. I would be more than happy to share what I learn with our fellow Press Club members at an in-person training event. Thank you for your consideration. I look forward to the banquet. Sincerely, Logan Finney ###
